text.title: Solo X Analytics Review Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Solo X Analytics Review application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Review MUST cover exactly five metrics (impressions, profile visits, net followers, replies-from-strangers, link clicks); follower count alone as headline is rejected.
rule.rationale: Impressions and follower count move together for bot-attracting content; replies-from-strangers + link clicks separate audience-growth from engagement theatre.

rule.statement: Total review time MUST be ≤20 minutes per week on a fixed day; overrunning means cutting depth, not extending.
rule.rationale: Analytics expand to fill any slot; cap forces signal-over-thorough.

rule.statement: Outlier = impressions ≥3x trailing-4-week median; non-outliers MUST NOT be studied as deeply.
rule.rationale: Studying every post mistakes randomness for signal.

rule.statement: Each review MUST produce exactly one named experiment with hypothesis + variable + success metric; zero or multiple experiments are rejected.
rule.rationale: Multiple simultaneous variable changes destroy signal; one-at-a-time builds the mental model.

rule.statement: Net followers MUST be tagged % qualified vs unqualified; chasing total-follower numbers without qualified discipline is rejected.
rule.rationale: Algorithm-bait attracts unqualified followers at high rates and dilutes pipeline.
